ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #38, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for (petitioner) Wells Fargo Bank.

The court stated that we are here for the 38th, 39th, 40th & 41st accountings and that there is question regarding compliance with the financial code. Ms. Boessenecker stated that this has been brought up previously by the examiners, that moving forward it will not be an issue, they are due a fee for the investment management, that the fees are reasonable and explained that the trust department changed during the 38th accounting, they are now on track, the portfolio has increased 23% and the goal is to distribute income. Ms. Boessenecker reported that there are 4 beneficiaries, all are charities, that the trust will go on indefinitely and responded to questions regarding the percentage that Wells Fargo is charging. The petition for accounting was taken under submission subject to review of the order approving the accounting but not approving the failure to comply with the financial code or the overage of $201.96.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #36, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for Wells Fargo Bank.

Ms. Boessenecker stated that Wells Fargo received trustee's fees, management fees for the mutual fund account and that they received no additional fees on their own assets. In addition, she stated that Wells Fargo would comply with sending notice on the management fee request. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
